name: Octreotide
executiveSummary: Somatostatin analog for variceal bleeding, fistulas, and neuroendocrine syndromes.
indications:
## Indications

- Esophageal variceal hemorrhage (adjuvant).
- High-output pancreatic and enteric fistulas.
- Neuroendocrine tumors.

## Precautions

- Cholelithiasis with prolonged use. Hyperglycemia/hypoglycemia.

> Adjust according to institutional protocols and medical prescription.

adulto.dose: Bolus 50 mcg IV then infusion 50 mcg/h for variceal bleeding (per protocol).

pediatrico.finalConcentration: Undiluted for bolus; intermittent in 50–200 mL.
pediatrico.infusionRate: 3-minute bolus in medical emergencies; otherwise 15 to 30 minutes with infusion pump.
pediatrico.dose: For gastrointestinal bleeding (GIB): initial bolus 1 mcg/kg, followed by continuous infusion of 1 mcg/kg/h.
pediatrico.notes: Antisecretory, antidiarrheal. May cause hyper- and hypotension, hyper- and hypoglycemia, headache, arrhythmias, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea. Store in refrigerator.

stability:
## General

- Infusion 24 h depending on dilution.

## Pediatric chart

- 4 days at room temperature once diluted. Store in refrigerator.
adverseEffects:
## Effects

- Nausea, abdominal pain, bradycardia, alteration of blood glucose.
